title: Design Principle
description: Experiential intent for a domain or job. Informs judgment, never mechanically constrains. Captures the why behind design decisions so it survives team changes.

title.description: A memorable statement of the principle. Not a rule — a quality.

job.description: The job this principle serves. Optional but encouraged.

intent.description: What experience quality this principle is trying to protect. Written as observable user feeling, not design prescription.

anti-patterns.description: Specific things to avoid. More useful than positive prescriptions because they preserve creative space while capturing real failures.

what-good-looks-like.description: A reference point, not a spec. May point to a Figma frame, a written description, or an existing screen that gets it right.

owner-team.description: Design team or equivalent. Not an individual.

informs.description: Requirements or design specs this principle should be considered alongside.

legitimacy.description: Design principles require design team approval, not just product owner approval.
